subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Clearly defines the key concept of change and revisits it in multiple sections
Strong organizational focus with Samsung-specific examples (marketâshare, advertising, product placement)
weaknesses: Analysis remains largely declarative rather than deeply woven around âchangeâ
Connections between volatility, SWOT findings, and the key concept are superficial in places
Early definition of change is generic and could tie more explicitly to internal/external factors
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates a good understanding of âchangeâ with clear analytical thinking (Band 3) and strong organizational links (Band 4). However, integration is not fully sustained across every analytical step, and some reflections lack depth. A holistic score of 3 reflects solid performance with room to more tightly weave the concept throughout.

strengths: Five documents supplied, meeting the 3â5 requirement
Sources (marketâshare report, survey, Olympic placement article) are relevant and anchor quantitative and qualitative insights
Evidence from supporting documents is effectively cited (e.g. marketâshare percentages)
weaknesses: Depth variesâsome articles are purely descriptive rather than analytical
Lacks the topâband ârange of ideasâ across more theoretical or peerâreviewed reports
Data presentation (survey results) could be more reader-friendly (e.g. chart or table)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student provides an appropriate number of relevant, in-depth documents that strengthen analysis (Band 3). While generally sufficient, the uneven depth and limited range of analytical sources prevent a 4; a score of 3 accurately reflects performance.

strengths: Selection of Ansoff Matrix, SWOT, and descriptive statistics is adequate and relevant to growth strategy
Clear mixedâmethods methodology and effective SWOT table presentation
Tool applications (Ansoff discussion, SWOT synthesis) tie back to Samsungâs context
weaknesses: Application lacks deeper numerical manipulation or integration into final recommendations
Citation practices need improvement (e.g. formal in-text statistical references)
Some claims (e.g. product-placement intrusiveness) are uncited, reducing rigor
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Tools are appropriately selected and generally applied to the research question (Band 3), but depth and academic rigor fall short of the highest band. A score of 3 reflects effective but not exemplary application.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Uses multiple secondary and primary data sets (marketâshare figures, survey results, placement counts)
Analysis links data to strategic insights (campaign impact on brand value)
Problem statement frames the use of survey data effectively
weaknesses: Analysis remains at a surface level with limited comparative or financial evaluation
Sampling details for primary data are omitted, limiting validity assessment
SWOT quadrant content is incomplete, and risk evaluation is underdeveloped
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Data selection and use meet an adequate standard, and analysis moves beyond description but lacks sustained integration and depth (Band 3). A score of 3 reflects competent execution with clear avenues for deeper evaluative work.

strengths: Conclusion explicitly answers the research question and aligns fully with evidence
Acknowledges limitations and reflects on strategic implications
weaknesses: Discussion of diversification and further research is brief and could be expanded
Could more tightly link final strategic recommendations back to the key concept of change
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion is fully consistent with the evidence and directly answers the research question, meeting the top band (Band 3). While reflective, it could deepen discussion on future avenues; 3 is the maximum and appropriate here.

strengths: Logical report structure with title page, TOC, methodology, analysis, and conclusion
Clear headings, consistent pagination, and coherent flow throughout
weaknesses: Minor formatting issues in the table of contents (empty row)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The organization is appropriate throughout (Band 2). Minor formatting improvements are suggested, but the structure fully meets rubric expectations, warranting a score of 2.

strengths: All required IA elements are present (title page with word count, TOC, references, appendices)
Consistent bibliography formatting and complete citation details
weaknesses: Figure numbering is misaligned between text and captions
Some online sources lack access dates in the bibliography
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation includes all mandatory elements with consistent formatting (Band 2). Small technical fixes remain, but the work satisfies the highest presentation standard, earning a 2.

strandSelections.reasoning: The report follows a logical structure with a title page, table of contents, methodology, analysis, and conclusion.  Headings are clear and pagination is present, satisfying the âappropriate structure throughoutâ descriptor (band 2).

The chosen documents (IDC market-share report, survey, Olympic placement article, SWOT article, textbook) are generally relevant and provide usable qualitative and quantitative detail.  Depth, however, is unevenâsome sources are descriptive web articles rather than analytical reportsâso the evidence aligns with ârelevant and sufficiently in-depthâ rather than the richest ârange of ideasâ top band.

All required IA elementsâtitle page with word count and RQ, table of contents, references, appendices with highlighted documentsâare present; thus the work qualifies for the highest presentation band.
Five supporting documents are listed and appended, satisfying the rubric requirement of 3-5 documents.

Ansoff and SWOT are applied directly to Samsung, and statistics are cited to support points about advertising and product placement.  Application is mostly correct and tied to the research question, though depth is limited (e.g. minimal numerical manipulation, no integration of SWOT findings into final judgement).  This fits ârelevance not always clear but adequately applied.â

The commentary uses several data sets (market-share chart, shipment figures, survey results) and explains what they indicate about Samsung.  Selection is adequate but not extensive; data are mainly secondary and descriptive.  Hence the work meets, but does not exceed, the âadequate selection and use of dataâ level.

Analysis moves beyond descriptionâlinking campaigns to brand value changes and discussing product-placement benefitsâyet it remains surface-level, with limited comparative or financial evaluation.  Integration of evidence is present but not sustained, aligning with âsome analysis of research questionâ (band 3) rather than the mostly effective/top levels.

The conclusion explicitly states to what extent the strategies have been effective and justifies this with the analytical findings, directly addressing the research question; therefore it sits in the top band for this strand.

The student selects standard but appropriate tools (Ansoff Matrix, SWOT, basic descriptive statistics).  While not extensive, this constitutes an adequate toolkit that is clearly related to growth strategy analysis, matching the âadequate selectionâ descriptor.

A clear, concise conclusion answers the research question and is broadly consistent with the evidence presented (advertising, product placement, survey results).  Limitations are acknowledged, demonstrating reflection.  This meets the highest band available for conclusions (3 marks).

Samsung is consistently at the centre of every section.  Market-share statistics, survey data, advertising campaigns, and Olympic product placements are all specific to the firm, and tool application (Ansoff, SWOT) is explicitly tied to Samsungâs context.  Links are made in most paragraphs, giving a strongâthough not flawlessâlevel of integration with the organisation, meriting the âstrong analysis with partial integration throughoutâ band.

The student clearly defines the key concept of change, explains its relevance to market-penetration strategies, and revisits the concept at several points (Ansoff discussion, advertising, product-placement sections and conclusion).  However, the discussion is mostly declarative (e.g. âthis encompasses the concept of changeâ¦â) rather than deeply woven into each analytical step.  The concept guides the commentary but is not fully integrated throughout every argument and piece of evidence, so the work aligns best with the âgood understanding with clear analytical thinkingâ level rather than the âfully integratedâ top band.

annotations.comment: The title page clearly states the research question, providing a focused research direction for the investigation.
While the key concept âChangeâ is stated on the title page, it lacks immediate context; consider briefly defining its relevance to Samsungâs strategies here.
The table of contents provides clear navigation with relevant page numbers, reinforcing a coherent report structure.
An empty row appears in the ToC between entries; remove or populate it to avoid confusion and ensure completeness.
Consider numbering sub-sections under Methodology consistently to reinforce hierarchical clarity in the ToC.
Context integrates marketâshare data (20.8%, 60.1M shipments) from Supporting Document 2 effectively, anchoring analysis in quantitative evidence.
Definition of âchangeâ is generic; refine it to explicitly link internal or external factors driving Samsungâs strategic adaptations.
In-text reference says âSee Figure 2â but caption labels it as Figure 1; align numbering for consistent visual referencing.
The problem statement clearly frames Samsungâs challenge and integrates survey data, effectively setting up the analysis.
Volatility is described but not tied back to the key concept of change; embed reflection on how such volatility drives strategic shifts.
The 10% year-on-year growth statistic should be formally cited in-text, rather than in parentheses, to strengthen academic rigor.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Methodology clearly outlines a mixed-methods approach and names specific tools (Ansoff Matrix, SWOT, descriptive statistics).
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Primary data description omits sampling details (demographics, selection method), limiting assessment of representativeness.
Justify why a 200-participant survey yields sufficient statistical power or discuss how sampling bias is mitigated.
Ansoff Matrix explanation is accurate and references the course companion, demonstrating effective tool selection.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The connection between the Ansoff discussion and the concept of change is superficial; map specific strategies to types of organizational change.
Integrate numerical data (e.g., campaign ROI, shipment increases) into the strategic discussion to quantify impact.
Survey results are used to support campaign effectiveness, adding empirical depth to strategic evaluation.
The 72% familiarity for âThe Next Big Thingâ campaign is cited but not contextualized; explain its strategic relevance.
Present survey findings in a chart or table to improve clarity and facilitate comparison across campaigns.
Product placement discussion includes a precise count (1,238 appearances), demonstrating dataâdriven analysis.
Claim that product placement is âless intrusiveâ lacks citation; recommend referencing consumer psychology research on placement effectiveness.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Discuss potential negative consumer reactions to product placement to present a balanced evaluation of its risks.
SWOT table effectively synthesizes strengths and weaknesses, summarizing Samsungâs strategic position.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: SWOT layout omits detailed content for opportunities and threats quadrants; ensure all four segments are fully populated.
Link each SWOT finding back to the research question and key concept to demonstrate integrated analysis.
Conclusion explicitly addresses the research question and acknowledges limitations, demonstrating reflective evaluation.
Further research into diversification is only briefly noted; expand on potential methods or scope for deeper insight.
Reframe the conclusion to explicitly tie strategic outcomes back to the concept of change for an integrated finish.
The bibliography presents all required elements and follows a consistent format, satisfying presentation standards.
Some online sources lack access dates in bibliography entries; ensure every web reference includes access information for consistency.
